name: Israel
summary: Day 62 Wednesday. IDF KINETIC DAY 3 — DEFENSE-MINISTRY-CONTRACTOR KILLED BY HEZBOLLAH DRONE TUE; LITANI BRIDGES DESTROYED; ISRAEL CONSPICUOUSLY SILENT ON IRAN-TRACK. Civilian contractor working for Israel's Defense Ministry was killed Tuesday April 28 by Hezbollah drone in southern Lebanon; 19-year-old son lightly injured (CONFIRMED — Haaretz, Times of Israel, UPI). Contractors were working on demolition of buildings in southern Lebanese villages on behalf of an Israeli civilian company contracted by the Defense Ministry. PARTIAL TRIGGER ACTIVATION case — strict reading does NOT activate the 'Israeli civilian casualty' trigger (operational casualty in active war zone, not civilian struck inside Israel proper); maximalist reading DOES activate. Operative tag: edge weight increment 0.5-1.0 (partial activation), trigger remains at WATCHING (Day 4 of 14, closes 2026-05-11). The IDF struck 20+ Hezbollah-linked sites across Beqaa Valley and southern Lebanon Mon-Tue. Israel's main bridges on the Litani River were BLOWN UP to cut south Lebanon from rest of country — geographic-expansion confirmation. Northern Israeli-towns school cancellations Tue but NO ROCKET HAS FALLEN ON ISRAELI CIVILIANS. Netanyahu admitted Israel is 'struggling to neutralize the threat of Hezbollah's 122 rockets and drones' but emphasized IDF freedom of action 'north of the Litani.' ON IRAN TRACK: Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post leading with IDF Lebanon strikes — conspicuously silent on the Iran question at government level. Times of Israel Apr 29 liveblog opened with London-Golders Green stabbing of two Jews (UK domestic anti-Semitism story, NOT Iran-related), suggesting Israeli mainstream media's primary lens is Lebanon front + diaspora protection, NOT Iran. Foreign Policy 'Israel Coordinating Potential Attack Plans with US' (Apr 21) reporting has NOT generated follow-through coverage in Israeli mainstream. Netanyahu is letting Trump own the Iran channel while Israel runs the Lebanon front — consistent with March-April pattern. The silence is durable. Israel Hayom 'All eyes on Pakistan' framing positioned Israel as a watcher; that posture has not been corrected. Israel is being marginalised in the Iran diplomatic track and is not pushing back via its usual Haaretz-military-correspondent leak channels. The IDF said Monday it struck more than 20 Hezbollah-linked infrastructure sites — weapons manufacturing, storage, rocket-launch (Haaretz live blog, Times of Israel). Lebanese Health Ministry: 14 killed, 37 wounded in Sunday April 26 retaliation strikes. Netanyahu said Hezbollah is 'trying to derail' talks and Israel will respond 'vigorously.' Critically, the Hezbollah lethal strike on Sgt. Fooks Sunday HAS NOT yet been followed by a Hezbollah strike causing an Israeli civilian casualty — the 14-day Israeli-civilian-casualty trigger (by 2026-05-11) is still WATCHING. The Lebanon vertical also has NOT spread horizontally: no Hezbollah surface-to-surface against Israeli population centres, no IRGC direct engagement. ON IRAN TRACK: Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post leading with IDF Lebanon strikes — and conspicuously silent on the Iran question at government level. Haaretz military correspondents have NOT leaked any IDF push for direct kinetic on Iran; Israel is being marginalised in the diplomatic track post-Trump cancellation and is marking that marginalization through ABSENCE on the Iran question. Israel Hayom Apr 23 NYT-Mojtaba reporting (prosthetic leg, plastic surgery, severe burns making speech difficult; access 'extremely difficult and limited'; communication via handwritten messages in sealed envelopes) is the most operationally-significant Israeli-media item still circulating. UN human rights office said both Israeli strikes AND Hezbollah rocket fire 'may breach international law.' Trump-brokered legal extension running through May 14 holds in legal architecture but is operationally voided. PRIOR DAY-1 SUMMARY: Sunday Apr 26 Sgt. Fooks killed in Hezbollah drone strike; IDF retaliated, killing 14 Lebanese. Sunday April 26 morning Hezbollah explosive drone attack killed Sgt. Idan Fooks (19, 7th Armored Brigade's 77th Battalion, from Petah Tikva) in southern Lebanon — first IDF-soldier death by direct Hezbollah attack since the November ceasefire. One officer + three troops seriously wounded; one moderate, one light. The two prior fatalities since the ceasefire were from pre-planted IEDs assessed as not remotely detonated; Sunday's drone is an active, deliberate strike — the qualitative threshold the November architecture was meant to suppress. IDF responded Sunday afternoon with airstrikes and artillery shelling north of Israeli-declared security zone — Lebanese Health Ministry confirms 14 killed, 37 wounded. Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of 'repeatedly violating the truce' and 'vowed to act forcefully in response.' Israel's Home Front Command issued gathering restrictions in northern border towns from Sunday 22:30 to Monday April 27 20:00 IDT — first time gathering restrictions have been formally imposed since the November ceasefire, itself an operational signal of expected Hezbollah escalation Monday. Trump-brokered ceasefire extension running through May 14 holds in legal architecture, but the substantive ceasefire has collapsed. ON IRAN TRACK: Israeli press silent on Iran at government level; Haaretz military-source lines suggest IDF senior officers uncomfortable with Trump's diplomatic-track marginalisation of Israel. Conspicuous absence: no senior-Israeli-source leak on a coordinated US-Israel kinetic option for Iran post the Trump cancellation, despite Trump's Saturday cancellation + Sunday 'nuclear dust' + Monday Putin-Araghchi meeting being precisely the trifecta that should produce a Netanyahu cabinet leak. The silence is durable. Israel Hayom's Saturday 'All eyes on Pakistan' framing positioned Israel as a watcher; that posture has not been corrected Sunday-Monday. Israel is being marginalised in the Iran diplomatic track and is not pushing back via its usual Haaretz-military-correspondent leak channels.

signals.content: Israel's Defense Minister Israel Katz confirmed 'preemptive' strike on Iran. Declared nationwide state of emergency. First phase planned for 4 days targeting senior leadership including Khamenei.
IDF launched ground incursion into southern Lebanon against Hezbollah. Described as 'forward defence' measure. Separate from Iran air campaign.
US struck Kharg Island. Israel struck nuclear sites (Natanz, Fordow, Isfahan) multiple times. Fourth targeted attack on nuclear facilities.
Israel struck South Pars gas field (world's largest). Major escalation into energy infrastructure. Trump denied advance knowledge.
Iran launched 5 missile salvos at Jerusalem and northern Israel within one hour. Struck towns near Dimona nuclear site, 180+ wounded.

Israel assassinated IRGC Navy chief Tangsiri at Bandar Abbas March 26 — confirmed by IDF and CENTCOM. Netanyahu hailed killing of 'the man who led closure of Hormuz.' Defense Minister Katz vowed to 'intensify and expand' attacks. Separately, first Houthi ballistic missile intercepted over Beersheba March 28 — no casualties. (Sources: IDF, CENTCOM, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, CNN, Jerusalem Post)

Iranian missiles struck Tel Aviv commercial street — Vyacheslav Vidmant (52) killed, 11+ injured, 8 impact sites including a university. Extensive building damage within 150m radius. Houthis launched two separate attacks on Israel within 24 hours — both intercepted, demonstrating sustained multi-front bombardment (Iran direct + Houthi from Yemen + Hezbollah from Lebanon). IDF killed 2 top Hezbollah communications unit members in Beirut overnight. CENTCOM struck Houthi positions in Yemen in response to Israel attacks. Israel expanding ground operations in southern Lebanon. (Sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, CNN, Defense News)

IDF confirmed targeting Malek Ashtar University and Imam Hossein University (IRGC-linked military research). IDF launched 'widespread' strikes across Tehran Saturday evening — dozens of infrastructure targets. Iran army targeted Haifa military aerospace complex and Ben Gurion airport fuel storage. Iran fired 6 missile salvos at Israel Saturday. Multi-front bombardment continues: Iran direct + Houthi from Yemen + Hezbollah from Lebanon. (Sources: IDF statement, CNN, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel)

EVENING UPDATE: Fire reported at Bazan oil refinery in Haifa following Iranian missile attack (Al Jazeera liveblog). Bazan is Israel's largest oil refinery. If confirmed, significant hit to Israel's energy infrastructure. Israel intercepted drones from Yemen (Houthi). Limited details on Bazan damage extent. Trump told Times of Israel that killing Iran's leaders 'truly is regime change' and deal 'could be soon.' (Sources: Al Jazeera liveblog, Times of Israel)

Three major developments: (1) IDF struck 170 targets 'throughout Tehran' in 24 hours including weapons production sites. Isfahan nuclear site struck — houses ~540 kg 60%-enriched uranium. (2) Iran missiles continued hitting central Israel, 8 lightly hurt. Missile rate 10-15/day vs 90/day Day 1. (3) Hezbollah unleashed 600+ attacks in 24 hours — doubling prior single-day high (~300). 4 IDF soldiers killed in southern Lebanon. Israel scaling back top missile defenses due to interceptor depletion (JPost). Haaretz: Netanyahu ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon.' Arms production tripled. Jewish opposition more than doubled (4%→11.5%), 'strongly support' down 74%→68%. WSJ exit report may accelerate Netanyahu's strikes. CONFIRMED (multiple independent sources).

Israel accelerating military campaign alongside peace signals: IDF struck 170 targets 'throughout Tehran' in 24 hours. Unnamed Israeli official: 'no intention to scale back before talks.' Haaretz reported Netanyahu ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon' — suggesting Israel is maximizing military damage while the diplomatic window exists. This diverges sharply from Trump's '2-3 weeks' exit timeline and Pezeshkian's peace overture. Israel pursuing its own objectives independent of US diplomatic posturing. The gap: Trump says 'great progress,' Israel says 'no scale-back' — allies not coordinating exit strategy. If ceasefire arrives, Israel wants maximum damage already inflicted. (Sources: IDF, Reuters, Al Jazeera, Haaretz, Business Standard)

Israel killed Haj Youssef Ismail Hashem — commander of Hezbollah's southern front — in Beirut airstrike April 1. Seven killed, 26 wounded. Most senior Hezbollah kill since war began March 2. Hezbollah confirmed the death. (Sources: Al Jazeera, Hezbollah statement)

IDF confirmed '170 targets in 24 hours throughout Tehran' on April 1-2. Unnamed Israeli official: 'no intention to scale back' before talks. Netanyahu previously ordered escalation 'in case war ends soon' per WSJ. Targeting now includes Iranian steel plants (Mobarakeh, Khouzestan) and pharmaceutical facilities — new category of economic infrastructure destruction. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CBS, NPR)

IDF entering 'decisive phase': all high-priority pre-war targets likely eliminated by April 3 (Wednesday). 60-70% of identified sites damaged. IDF planning 3 MORE weeks of operations to systematically degrade Iran's defense industry. Target categories: critical (ballistic missile, nuclear-linked), essential (broader military-industrial network), satellite/space capabilities. Iran fired missiles at Israel — homes and cars damaged. Total casualty toll in Iran: 2,076 killed, 26,500 wounded since Feb 28. (Sources: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Time)

IDF struck 200+ Iranian targets and 140+ Hezbollah sites as of April 5. Total: 4,000+ targets, 10,000+ components (IDF BG Defrin). 60-70% of pre-war high-priority targets damaged. 3+ weeks more operations planned. In Lebanon: killed 2 girls, wounded 40 near Tyre April 4. Hospital damaged. IDF soldier killed by friendly fire. Lebanon total: 1,001 killed, 2,584 wounded. (Sources: ANI, Times of Israel, Al Jazeera)

CONFIRMED: Haifa missile strike — 2 dead recovered from rubble, 2 still trapped. IDF assessed CLUSTER BOMB WARHEAD — significant escalation in warhead type. Four others wounded. Rescue operation ongoing with Haifa Fire & Rescue, IDF Home Front Command, Magen David Adom. ~170 ballistic missiles launched in April 4-5 window — 6 on Friday, 8 on Saturday. Half targeting northern Israel, 5 toward center, one each south and Jerusalem. Separate Tel Aviv residential strike killed civilian woman in forties, injured 27. BAZAN refinery in Haifa hit in same salvo. Total war hospital evacuations: 6,833.

Lebanon strikes escalating: 15+ killed April 5-6. Beirut Jnah: 5 killed, 52 wounded (~100m from Rafik Hariri Hospital). Ain Saadeh: 3 killed. Kfar Hatta: 7 killed including 4-year-old girl. IDF Chief Lt. Gen. Zamir visited troops in southern Lebanon, pledged to 'intensify strikes.' Masnaa border crossing with Syria FORCED CLOSED after Israeli threat — civilians now queuing at Qaa northern crossing. Lebanon total: 1,460+ killed (Health Ministry).

CLAIMED (Hezbollah): Cruise missile fired at Israeli warship 68nm off Lebanese coast at midnight April 6. Hezbollah claims 'direct hit confirmed' after hours of surveillance. FIRST anti-ship cruise missile claim by Hezbollah since war began. IDF told AFP: 'We are not aware of it.' Unverified videos circulating. If confirmed, major capability escalation — C4ISR sophistication at 68nm range. IDF denial should not be treated as final given F-15 shootdown was eventually confirmed after initial ambiguity.

Israel struck Iran's South Pars petrochemical plant (largest, half of petrochemical output) and Mahshahr petrochemical complex (Fajr 1/2 utility plants destroyed, all production shut) — first sustained strike campaign directly targeting Iran's energy/industrial infrastructure. Defense Minister Katz confirmed. Also struck three Tehran airports (dozens of helicopters/aircraft destroyed). IRGC Intelligence Chief Brigadier General Khademi killed in precision strike — most significant intelligence decapitation since Tangsiri. Quds Force commander Bagheri also eliminated. IDF Northern Command chief Milo admitted Israel overestimated damage to Hezbollah — 'significant gap' between assessed and actual capabilities. Hezbollah retains hundreds of launchers and tens of thousands of rockets. IDF deems militarily disarming Hezbollah 'unrealistic' — new objective: 2-3km security zone. Haaretz: IDF 'stretched to breaking point.' Haifa cluster bomb final count: 4 dead (Gershovich family + partner). 210+ Hezbollah missiles into Israel since March 2, ~100 during Passover. (Sources: Euronews, i24News, Military.com, IDF, Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post, FDD)

SOUTH PARS PETROCHEMICAL HIT FOR SECOND TIME + IRGC INTEL CHIEF DECAPITATION [CONFIRMED]. April 6 strike on the petrochemical plant inside South Pars natural gas field (world's largest, shared with Qatar). Defense Minister Katz on the record: 'a powerful strike on the largest petrochemical facility in Iran' — plant responsible for HALF Iran's petrochem output. Combined with prior week strike on #2 petrochem site, ~85% of Iran's petrochemical production now offline. Same operation killed Maj. Gen. Majid Khademi (IRGC Intel Chief — most senior IRGC intel loss of the war) and Asghar Bakeri (Quds Force undercover unit leader). Israel choosing to kill IRGC intel chief DURING ceasefire mediation is itself evidence of intent: Israel does not believe a deal will hold and is making one impossible.

NETANYAHU PHONED TRUMP WARNING AGAINST CEASEFIRE [REPORTED]. Sunday April 5, Netanyahu phoned Trump ostensibly to congratulate on F-15 crew rescue. Per Israeli reporting (Haaretz, Times of Israel), he in fact warned Trump that 'a ceasefire carries significant risks' and against agreeing to a truce at this stage. Trump reportedly told Netanyahu a truce remains possible IF US demands are met by Iran but 'stressed that he would not give up on his demand that Tehran hand over all of its enriched uranium and agree not to resume enrichment.' First public US confirmation that the maximalist nuclear demand remains the US ceiling. US-Israel alignment visibly fraying: Netanyahu wants escalation; Trump wants a deal but on terms Iran will never accept. The Israel-US split is the critical fault line.

HAARETZ 'ISRAELI POLITICAL SOURCE': US-Iran talks likely to fail; Israel prepares 'extensive attack.' Source almost certainly a Netanyahu war cabinet member. Confidence that talks will fail tracks the South Pars decapitation timing — Israel has war-gamed the ceasefire collapse and is staging force for an operation 'larger than current operations.' Tripartite salvo (Iran + Hezbollah + Houthis) intercepted by IDF April 6. Beirut southern suburbs (dahiya) and Christian town of Ain Saadeh hit overnight. Lt. Gen. Eyal Zamir announced widening of Operation Northern Arrows: 'The damage to Hezbollah will intensify.'

EVENING UPDATE: IDF executed pre-deadline 'wide-scale wave' — dozens of infrastructure sites struck hours before 8 PM ET. Three Tehran airports struck (dozens of helicopters/aircraft destroyed). Shiraz Marvdasht petrochemical complex hit. Persian-language railway warning issued to all Iran. Multiple bridges struck. 150+ aircraft involved. SABIC Jubail fire — missile debris fell near Saudi facilities during Israeli operations, most significant Saudi energy infrastructure attack of war. Rafi-Nia synagogue in Tehran 'completely destroyed' — Iran will use for propaganda. IAEA 'deeply concerned' by strikes near Bushehr — 4th incident, 1 staff killed. 98th Division deployed to southern Lebanon for 'forward defensive posture.' 91st Division killed Hezbollah anti-tank operators. Netanyahu phoned Trump warning against ceasefire (REPORTED, Haaretz). Haaretz 'Israeli political source': US-Iran talks likely to fail; Israel prepares 'extensive attack.' (Sources: ABC News, Al Jazeera, Arab News, Middle East Eye, Haaretz, Times of Israel)

ISRAEL BACKS BILATERAL US-IRAN CEASEFIRE — BUT EXCLUDES LEBANON [CONFIRMED]. Netanyahu's position evolved within 24 hours: he initially urged Trump NOT to agree to the ceasefire (his Sunday April 5 phone call to Trump warned against it), then BACKED the two-week pause after Trump's announcement, but explicitly stated the deal 'does not include Lebanon' and reaffirmed continued operations against Hezbollah. Earlier, Netanyahu reportedly told senior officials Saturday that Hezbollah operations 'would not be halted even if Iran and the United States reached a ceasefire.' This Lebanon exclusion is the structural risk to the entire deal: Iran's 10-point plan demanded 'end of war across all fronts' including Hezbollah, Houthis, and Iraqi PMF. The bilateral US-Iran ceasefire as structured does not deliver this. If Israel strikes Beirut on Day 1 of negotiations, Iran's hardliners will frame the deal as bad-faith and pressure to withdraw. The IDF struck a Christian mountain town (Ain Saadeh) east of Beirut on April 6, killing a prominent anti-Hezbollah politician and his wife. Operations continue.

IDF executed 'most intense day of strikes since Day One' on April 7 BEFORE the ceasefire — South Pars #2 petrochemical, three Tehran airports, Shiraz petrochemical complex, bridges, railways, plus the Khademi assassination [CONFIRMED]. Israeli operational objectives were largely achieved: ~85% Iranian petrochemical destruction, IRGC intelligence chief eliminated, Tehran airports degraded. Israel can accept the 2-week pause without giving up Hezbollah front. The Iranian cluster munition retaliation hit ~50 sites in central Israel, 6 wounded.

Israel launched 'Operation Eternal Darkness' on April 8 — 50 IAF fighter jets struck 100 Hezbollah targets across Lebanon in 10 minutes using approximately 160 munitions. Central Beirut struck WITHOUT prior warning. At least 254 killed, 1,165 wounded — the single deadliest day of the 2026 Lebanon war. Defense Minister Katz: 'Today Hezbollah has suffered its heaviest blow since the 2024 beeper operation.' This was the predicted failure mode flagged in the April 8 brief: 'Any IDF strike on Beirut during Friday Islamabad talks gives Iran's hardliners cause to claim bad faith.' It happened within 18 hours, not even waiting for Friday.

Netanyahu explicitly stated ceasefire 'does not include Lebanon,' reaffirmed continued operations against Hezbollah. Trump called Lebanon a 'separate skirmish.' Pakistan PM Sharif directly contradicted both, stating ceasefire covers 'everywhere including Lebanon' citing Iran's 10-point plan. This creates a three-way contradiction on ceasefire scope that is the structural fault line for Friday Islamabad talks.

Day 43. Israel struck ADDITIONAL targets in Lebanon on April 9 despite ceasefire — putting the truce 'in further jeopardy.' Operation Eternal Darkness toll revised UP: 254-300+ killed, 1,150 wounded (Lebanon Health Ministry). Hezbollah retaliated with rockets into northern Israel on April 9. Netanyahu authorized direct negotiations with Lebanon at State Department starting next week (Axios). Vance's offer to 'restrain Lebanon strikes during talks' appears not honored given April 9 continuation. France (Macron) entered scope dispute saying Lebanon 'fully included' — making it 3-vs-2 against US/Israel position. Netanyahu separately stated enriched uranium 'will be removed by agreement or in resumed fighting.' (Source: PBS, Axios, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel)

Day 44. Israel escalated 'negotiate while bombing' strategy. Nabatieh strike killed 13 Lebanese State Security personnel at government building — NOT Hezbollah, these are Lebanese government security forces. Largest attack on Nabatieh since war began. Also struck residential areas in Aita al-Shaab, al-Majadel (Tyre district), and Hanine. HRW reported Israeli strikes killed hundreds and damaged vital bridge. Hezbollah ESCALATED dramatically in response: 100+ rockets since ceasefire, 70 on April 10. Most significant: struck Ashdod Naval Base in central Israel (~200km from Lebanese border) — if confirmed, qualitative capability escalation from border harassment to strategic depth. Also struck Haifa (sirens across city), Kiryat Shmona (fire, no warning siren), Karmiel (4-month-old infant and 4 children injured), and Nahariya. Haaretz military correspondents report systemic gap between political rhetoric and military reality: IDF intelligence assesses Hezbollah maintains 'solid military hold in southern Lebanon,' IDF 'not planning to advance deeper' (halted 10km south of Litani), senior officers say 'disarming Hezbollah can only be done by Lebanon,' IDF 'stretched to breaking point.' Trump told Israeli journalist he instructed Netanyahu to keep operations 'low key' to avoid derailing Pakistan talks — a shift from 'not included' to implicit acknowledgment that Lebanon violence threatens the talks.

Day 45. Israeli strikes killed at least 18 people across southern Lebanon on April 11: at least 8 killed in a village near Sidon, and at least 10 (including 3 emergency workers) killed in Nabatieh district. IDF said it struck 200+ Hezbollah targets in 24 hours. THREE diplomatic developments: (1) Israel-Lebanon talks set for Tuesday at State Department between Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter and Lebanese counterpart — but Leiter explicitly stated Israel 'refused to discuss a ceasefire with the Hezbollah terrorist organization.' (2) Lebanon PM Nawaf Salam CANCELLED his planned Washington trip, citing need to 'safeguard the security of the Lebanese people and their unity' as Hezbollah supporters rallied in Beirut against negotiations. Hezbollah lawmaker Hassan Fadlallah rejected direct talks as violating Lebanon's constitutional framework. (3) Ghalibaf named Lebanon ceasefire as one of Iran's preconditions for any Islamabad deal — making Lebanon the structural link between the bilateral US-Iran track and the broader regional conflict. Total Lebanon toll since March 2: 2,020 killed, 6,436 wounded.

AFTERNOON UPDATE: Israel continued striking southern Lebanon overnight April 11-12, killing at least 11 in addition to the 13 security personnel in Nabatieh (death toll revised up). IDF confirmed striking "loaded and ready-to-launch rocket launcher" in Jouaiya. Operational tempo 200+ targets in 24 hours continues. Two rockets and one drone launched from Lebanon toward Israel -- sirens in Upper Galilee. Tuesday DC talks still set at ambassador level (downgraded from PM) after Salam cancelled Washington trip. Israel rejects discussing ceasefire with Hezbollah -- focusing on military degradation while keeping diplomatic appearance. Lebanon is where Israel has operational agency; Islamabad was background noise. (Sources: IDF statement, Al Jazeera, The National)

IDF raided Bint Jbeil hospital — killed ~20 Hezbollah fighters. Cut roads in southern Lebanon, used white phosphorus. Two IDF soldiers wounded April 13. IDF Chief Zamir ordered 'heightened state of readiness' after Islamabad talks failure. Israel-Lebanon ambassador-level talks still on for Tuesday at State Dept. Haaretz: 'Trump considering limited strikes on Iran' — if confirmed, major escalation of US direct involvement.

Israel-Lebanon ambassador-level talks confirmed for April 15 at State Department — Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter, Lebanese Ambassador Nada Hamadeh Moawad, US Ambassador to Lebanon Michel Issa leading. Fundamental disagreement on agenda: Lebanon says about declaring ceasefire; Israel says 'formal peace negotiations.' Netanyahu's top priority: disarming Hezbollah — a non-starter. Israel explicitly refused to discuss ceasefire with Hezbollah (source: Times of Israel, Al Jazeera, Washington Post, Haaretz). On the ground: IDF near-completion of Bint Jbeil capture, five divisions deployed, 2,055 Lebanese killed, 6,588 wounded, 1.2M displaced since March 2. Hezbollah deploying FPV drones from Ukraine-war tactics. Israeli media preparing public for resumed hostilities rather than resolution.

EVENING UPDATE: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will personally host and participate in the April 15 Israel-Lebanon talks at State Department — ELEVATING from ambassador-level to Secretary of State level. Most high-level direct Israel-Lebanon meeting since 1993 (CONFIRMED — Axios, State Department, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post). Participants: Rubio, senior adviser Michael Needham, US Ambassador to Lebanon Michel Issa, Israeli Ambassador Leiter, Lebanese Ambassador Hamadeh Moawad. Agenda: (1) immediate ceasefire, (2) Hezbollah disarmament, (3) full peace agreement. Rubio's presence signals Trump administration views this as priority — potentially because a Lebanon breakthrough could provide face-saving win offsetting Islamabad failure. The Iran connection: Ghalibaf named Lebanon ceasefire as one of Iran's preconditions at Islamabad — progress here could unlock broader US-Iran negotiation path.

CONFIRMED: First direct Israel-Lebanon diplomatic talks since 1993 occurred at State Department April 14-15, hosted by SecState Rubio personally. Participants: Rubio, senior adviser Needham, US Ambassador Issa, Israeli Ambassador Leiter, Lebanese Ambassador Hamadeh Moawad. Trilateral joint statement: 'agreed to launch direct negotiations at a mutually agreed time and venue' — process could curb Iran's influence and 'unlock significant reconstruction assistance and economic recovery for Lebanon.' Israel Hayom: 'historic.' Japan Times: 'slim odds of breakthrough.' Structural obstacle: Hezbollah's Naim Qassem pre-emptively rejected talks as 'futile,' Fadlallah declared Hezbollah 'will not abide by any agreements.' Israel wants formal peace/Hezbollah disarmament; Lebanon's most powerful armed group refuses to participate. (Sources: US State Department, Axios/Barak Ravid, Washington Times, CBC News, Al Jazeera, Japan Times, Israel Hayom)

CONFIRMED MAJOR ESCALATION: IDF strikes killed 182 Lebanese on Wednesday April 15 — HIGHEST single-day death toll of the Israel-Hezbollah war. Hezbollah launched 105 attack waves in response (Alma Research — most intense single day of 2026 war). Netanyahu announced Israel EXPANDING IDF-occupied area in Lebanon toward parts of the Syrian border and is 'ready to renew strikes on Iran.' IDF Chief of Staff Zamir has 'approved further plans' for both Lebanon and Iran operations — careful bureaucratic language (planning ≠ executing). Haaretz military correspondents flagging civilian-military divergence: IDF leadership more measured than political echelon; Haaretz reported IDF assesses Hezbollah unlikely to accept truce without Iran agreement — contradicts Trump's separate de-linking signal. 105-waves/182-deaths day is consistent with parties sharpening bargaining positions ahead of Round 2, not end-of-war de-escalation. (Sources: Times of Israel, Xinhua, Haaretz, Wikipedia 2026 Lebanon war timeline, Alma Research Center, Middle East Eye)

Haaretz reported senior US official told it Trump 'would welcome the end of hostilities in Lebanon as part of a peace agreement between Israel and Lebanon' even if 'this is not something we have asked for nor is it part of the peace negotiations with Iran' — a remarkable on-record US framing that DE-LINKS Lebanon from the Iran track, contrary to Ghalibaf's Islamabad insistence that Lebanon must be included. Simultaneously Haaretz reported IDF assesses Hezbollah unlikely to accept truce without Iran agreement — CONTRADICTS the first piece's premise. Israeli envoy to US separately told Haaretz Israel wants 'France as far away as possible from peace talks with Lebanon' — parallel friction on UK-France Hormuz/Lebanon initiative. Most important diplomatic signal of week that goes largely unnoticed: if US is prepared to pursue a US-Iran deal that does NOT include Lebanon, Ghalibaf's Islamabad preconditions become movable. Precisely the kind of concession pattern that materializes in Round 2 preparations. (Sources: Haaretz, Times of Israel)

CEASEFIRE DAY 1 — 10KM ZONE OPERATIONALIZED AS 'SELF-DEFENSE' FRAMEWORK [CONFIRMED]. The 10-day ceasefire took effect at midnight Beirut time April 16-17. Within hours, Lebanon's army reported Israeli violations: 'intermittent shelling of several southern Lebanese villages,' specifically artillery and machine-gun fire on Al-Khiam and Debbin, and drone attacks in the Western Bekaa region. IDF procedural response: 'reserves the right to take all necessary measures in self-defense' (CNN) — effectively pre-authorizing continued strikes under Netanyahu's 10km security zone framework, the same language Israel used pre-2024 to justify Lebanon airstrikes. Israeli coverage on April 17 subdued relative to the pre-ceasefire escalation; Times of Israel and Haaretz not running standalone April 17 liveblogs. Coalition-politics frame (Likud 28 Knesset seats, coalition short of majority): pause sold to Israeli right as 'timeout, not surrender.' No Haaretz military-correspondent scoop contradicting the official line — suggests IDF senior ranks aligned with government's 'retain operational freedom inside zone' posture. Israeli tone CONSTRAINED, not defiant: not celebrating, not conciliating. Iran FM Baghaei directly contested the 10km zone, demanding full Israeli withdrawal from south Lebanon — unmet publicly. (Sources: Al Jazeera, CNN April 17 liveblog, Lebanese Army, Iran MFA, Haaretz silence)

HAARETZ: 'TRUMP HIJACKED ISRAELI FOREIGN POLICY' — ESTABLISHMENT CRITIQUE OF NETANYAHU BOXED-IN POSITION [CONFIRMED; premium Haaretz analysis]. Haaretz April 17 analysis title: 'Trump Dictates the Terms in Lebanon and Iran, Leaving Netanyahu Boxed In — Trump hijacked Israeli foreign policy so Netanyahu had no say in Lebanon truce.' Sharpest Israeli-establishment critique of Netanyahu's diplomatic position since ceasefire announcement. Haaretz's military-correspondent sourcing tends to reflect senior IDF officer thinking that cabinet is suppressing — 'boxed in' language suggests IDF senior ranks see cabinet as lacking leverage. Complements Times of Israel 'Netanyahu shocked to learn of truce from media' (April 16, carried into April 17-18). Israeli coalition hawkish bloc restive: MK letters urging occupation-and-evacuation of south Lebanon (Haaretz April 5) — not aligned with Trump-imposed framework. Watch: cabinet instability, Smotrich/Ben-Gvir walkout threats, September 2026 deadline of Israel's security cabinet's original Lebanon strategy. Day 2 cabinet INTACT: no Finance Ministry or National Security Ministry resignation = hardline bloc accepting pause tactically; salient if 10-day pause transitions to permanent framework. Haaretz April 5: IDF 'admits it can't disarm Hezbollah' — supporting read that cabinet cannot execute stated objectives. Operational Day 2: IDF Kounine drone strike killed one (Lebanon Health Ministry via CBC News, Al Jazeera) — IDF characterised as 'self-defense.' Casualty-per-day rate ~1 = lowest sustained of war. Jerusalem Post / Times of Israel (more sympathetic to Netanyahu) do NOT contest the 'boxed in' framing. (Sources: Haaretz April 17 ME correspondent analysis, Haaretz April 5, Times of Israel liveblog April 18, CBC News)

ISRAELI MEDIA PIVOTED TO DOMESTIC COALITION + LEBANON HOLDING PATTERN; HORMUZ BELOW THE FOLD [CONFIRMED Sunday morning publication pattern]. Sunday April 19 morning Israeli press focus has shifted almost entirely to domestic Netanyahu-Smotrich-Ben-Gvir coalition dynamics and the Lebanon Day 3 holding pattern. Hormuz material is placed BELOW THE FOLD and framed as 'Iran's showdown with US' rather than an Israel-relevant event — Haaretz's Friday 'Trump hijacked Israeli foreign policy' frame carries through Sunday (Hormuz is now a Trump problem in the Israeli press). Haaretz Sunday morning liveblog leads with coalition dynamics and Lebanon. Times of Israel liveblog carries UKMTO operational updates factually with no editorial amplification. Jerusalem Post ran the IRGC-vs-Araghchi angle more prominently Saturday (April 18: 'Iran's IRGC seems to be fighting Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi') but Sunday morning is quiet. Ynet, Channel 12, Channel 13 — no standout Sunday morning material. Smotrich and Ben-Gvir have NOT walked out of coalition Day 2→3 — hardline bloc accepting pause tactically; no new Netanyahu cabinet leaks Sunday morning. The Mojtaba 'Navy's Ready' angle Haaretz floated Saturday has NOT been elevated or amplified by other Israeli outlets Sunday morning — single-source-attribution concern still stands; Mojtaba node activation should remain paused until a Persian-language primary surfaces. Israeli press has outsourced Hormuz to the US domain. Domestic politics dominate. (Sources: Haaretz Sunday liveblog, Times of Israel liveblog, Jerusalem Post April 18, Ynet)

WO (RES) BARAK KALFON KILLED BY HEZBOLLAH IED IN SOUTHERN LEBANON — FIRST IDF FATALITY UNDER 10-DAY CEASEFIRE [REPORTED multi-source: Times of Israel, JNS, Jerusalem Post, Ynet, The Media Line; sourcing tension on incident date — disclosure date Sunday is hard anchor]. Warrant Officer (res.) Barak Kalfon, 48, of 226th Reserve Paratroopers Brigade's 7056th Battalion (Adi, northern Israel), was killed in Jebbayn, southern Lebanon, when an IED detonated during a building-search operation. Three to nine other soldiers wounded (sourcing inconsistent — IDF spokesperson statement to be cross-checked). Kalfon was an engineer at Rafael. Times of Israel dates incident Friday April 17; The Media Line dates it Saturday April 18 ('one day after ceasefire'); public disclosure Sunday April 19. Kiryat Shmona on Israeli side: municipal protests against the ceasefire ran Sunday with a convoy to Jerusalem — domestic political pressure on Netanyahu from the north building. Defense ETF (ITA) Friday +1.3%; if a second incident this week surfaces, defense-budget pressure on Netanyahu re-engages and ITA likely +3-5% 1W.

LEBANON CEASEFIRE DAY 4 — KALFON IED FATALITY DETAILS UNCHANGED; NO IDF RETALIATION; HEZBOLLAH KINETIC RESTRAINT HOLDS [CONFIRMED]. Kalfon (Warrant Officer Barak Kalfon, 226th Reserve Paratroopers Brigade, killed in Jebbayn) remains the only Israeli fatality under the 10-day ceasefire. Defense Minister Katz's Sunday tribute ("brave and dedicated fighter… devoted family man… engineer at Rafael") surfaced with no IDF retaliatory strike. Hezbollah SG Qassem Sunday speech called the ceasefire "an insult to Lebanon" — RHETORIC only, no rockets. Structural reading holds at Day 4: low-signature kinetic permitted + escalatory rhetoric permitted + open rocket war withheld. Ceasefire fragility unchanged (Hezbollah non-signatory, IDF in-country, no withdrawal). April 26 expiry in 6 days.

ISRAELI MEDIA TONE: QUIET → GRIEF-FOCUSED ON KALFON FUNERAL [SOURCE TONE]. Monday Israeli outlets (Times of Israel, Ynet, Jerusalem Post, Haaretz) dominated by Kalfon funeral coverage — Katz tribute, Kiryat Shmona municipal convoy, 226th Reserve Paratroopers unit — displacing Hormuz framing to interior pages. Haaretz military correspondents have published nothing Sun night-Mon AM specifically challenging the government's Lebanon-ceasefire-holds framing (absence-as-intelligence: if IDF senior officers had private disagreement, would typically leak via Haaretz). Hegemonic Israeli frame for Hormuz/Touska: "Trump problem, Iran problem, Israel watches."

ISRAEL 'COORDINATING POTENTIAL ATTACK PLANS WITH US' ON IRAN TRACK [REPORTED, Times of Israel single-source]. TIO April 21 live blog: 'Israel doubts prospects of Iran deal, is coordinating potential attack plans with US.' Inverts Haaretz April 21 analytical pattern (dovish on Iran, pessimistic on Lebanon) — Israel is moving from 'decoupled-from-US-Iran-dovishness' to 'hedging-for-kinetic-if-talks-fail.' No Channel 12/13 or Haaretz IDF imminent-strike leak as of 11:30 IST Wed. Add watching trigger.

NETANYAHU KIRYA STATEMENT: ISRAEL WILL 'CONTINUE STRIKING IRAN IN COORDINATION WITH THE WHITE HOUSE' [REPORTED — Netanyahu public statement + REPORTED TIO single-source on coordination = together CONFIRMED on public-posture level]. Netanyahu Wednesday at the Kirya IDF HQ publicly consolidated the prior reported-only coordination signal. Defrin (IDF Spokesperson Brig. Gen.) confirmed 'the IDF was ready to attack Iran again if required' in same news cycle. With IRGC Wednesday kinetic action providing real-world ammunition for the 'Iran deal will fail' thesis, Netanyahu's coordination signalling has moved from contingency to imminent-relevance. Combined with Trump's 'hold our Attack' Tuesday disclosure + Trump 3-5 day private window (Axios) + IDF 'ready to attack' + IRGC kinetic = the most loaded the Israeli track has been since Operation Epic Fury. Watching trigger 'Channel 12/13 or Haaretz publishes IDF imminent-strike preparation on Iran track within 72 hours' is firing the 24-hour mark; check Thursday morning Israeli media. Defense complex underpriced reload risk: ITA -3.8% on a day Israel publicly reaffirmed strike-coordination is tactically mispriced (NOT a structural conviction signal — defense thesis played out at 28%-CAGR-3yr).
sources: Times of Israel 'Israel doubts prospects of Iran deal'

signals.content: CHANNEL 12/13/KAN COORDINATED LEAK — IDF COMBAT-READINESS PROCEDURES INITIATED [CONFIRMED — coordinated Hebrew TV reporting; multiple primary outlets]. The 72-hour 'Channel 12/13 or Haaretz publishes IDF imminent-strike preparation' watching trigger FIRES at the 24-hour mark. Times of Israel describes 'an apparent coordinated leak': (a) Channel 12 reports IDF 'gearing up for renewed conflict with Iran' AND preparing for 'a potential Iranian surprise attack' (two-track legitimising offensive AND defensive prep); (b) Kan public broadcaster cites a 'senior defense official' stating 'Israel is interested in renewing the war against Iran' because the war ended 'too early, without sufficient pressure being applied on Iran regarding the nuclear issue and ballistic missiles'; (c) Channel 13 reports IDF alert level raised 'significantly' AND Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir instructed the military 'to prepare for an immediate resumption of fighting and ordered the initiation of combat readiness procedures.' Three-channel simultaneity is the deliberate signal — Israeli defense establishment uses coordinated leaks when wanting both Iran (message received) and Washington (pressure to extend the weekend) to act on same signal. Netanyahu Wednesday Kirya statement + IDF Spokesperson Defrin 'ready to attack' + Thursday's coordinated leak = 72-hour staircase from contingency to combat readiness procedures initiated. Most structurally significant Israeli-side signal since Operation Epic Fury. Action vs rhetoric: PREPARATION (combat readiness procedures = operational step, not yet kinetic); leak itself is CONFIRMED RHETORIC/SIGNALING. Iranian response to be measured via IRGC/IRNA over next 24 hours. Haaretz Thursday liveblog carries the IEA 'biggest energy security threat in history' framing AND Lebanese journalist-killed-in-Israeli-airstrike side by side — institutional dove remains mildly sceptical but its own April 21 'Trump Threatens War to Force Peace as Iran Sends Conflicting Signals' now reads as prelude. Times of Israel naming the 'apparent coordinated leak' mechanism openly is itself intelligence about how Israeli journalists read their own defense establishment's signals.

LEBANON DAY 8 OF 10 — DIRECT WASHINGTON TALKS + HEZBOLLAH ROCKETS/DRONES + ISRAELI JOURNALIST KILLED [CONFIRMED]. Three Lebanon-track developments Thursday: (a) Lebanese Ambassador to US Nada Hamadeh Moawad meets Israeli Ambassador Yechiel Leiter in Washington for second round of direct talks in three decades — US-brokered mechanism to extend the 10-day truce beyond Sunday April 26 expiry is now explicit diplomacy, not backchannel; (b) Democracy Now April 22: Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Israeli forces FOR THE FIRST TIME SINCE THE TRUCE TOOK EFFECT — ceasefire publicly fraying; (c) Israeli demolition operations continue with paramedics recovering body of Lebanese journalist hours after an Israeli airstrike in southern Lebanon Thursday morning; (d) Israeli officials continue to state troops will remain inside southern Lebanon during any truce extension. Structurally, April 26 IST now carries TWO simultaneous expiries: Trump 3-5 day Iran window AND Lebanon 10-day ceasefire — both depend on US mediation success; both have parallel military-readiness escalations (IDF combat-readiness procedures on Iran track; Israeli airstrikes on Lebanese journalist track). Stacking of two expiries into one weekend materially increases the probability of kinetic escalation in the window.

COMBAT-READINESS PROCEDURES HOLD — NO FRIDAY KINETIC; HAARETZ FRAMING SHIFTED TO 'TRUMP IS FED UP' [CONFIRMED non-events + REPORTED framing shift]. The Channel 12/13/Kan-leaked combat-readiness procedures remain active. No Friday kinetic by Israel against Iran. Haaretz's Friday headline — 'Trump Is Fed Up With the Iran War. That's No Guarantee He Won't Restart It' — is notably a shift in framing FROM Thursday's 'imminent strike' leak TO a more analytical framing about restart conditions. This is consistent with Israeli defense establishment holding posture but not escalating in front of Trump weekend window. Haaretz's characteristic pattern: when IDF operational track has shifted, Haaretz's military-desk framing shifts first. Netanyahu has not made a new public statement. Times of Israel reiterated IDF '3 more weeks of operations to systematically degrade Iran's defense industry' framing but without new specifics. Hezbollah Shtula rocket salvo + Israeli Yater airstrike continue the Lebanon-track kinetic. No new movements in Iraq, Syria, or against Iranian nuclear facilities reported.
sources: CNN Iran war live blog April 24
signals.verification: CONFIRMED combat-readiness posture; CONFIRMED no kinetic Friday; REPORTED on framing shift.

signals.content: ISRAEL-LEBANON CEASEFIRE EXTENDED 3 WEEKS — TRUMP WHITE HOUSE ANNOUNCEMENT [CONFIRMED ACTION]. Second round State Department direct talks (Moawad-Leiter) produced 3-week extension announced by Trump Thursday. Runs to ~May 14. Israeli troops remain in southern Lebanon during extension. Israeli airstrike on Yater Thursday followed by Hezbollah rocket salvo on Shtula settlement — kinetic layer persists. Wafiq Safa (Hezbollah): group will not recognise Washington agreements. PM Salam (Lebanon): any deal requires full Israeli withdrawal from buffer zone. April 26 double-expiry architecture dismantled; only Iran track expires this weekend.

IDF STRUCK 6+ SOUTHERN LEBANON LOCATIONS OVERNIGHT — KINETIC EROSION OF EXTENDED CEASEFIRE [CONFIRMED ACTION]. The IDF announced Saturday morning it had struck Hezbollah targets across southern Lebanon overnight, naming Al Saamiya, Reman, and Deir El Zahrani in its official statement. Lebanese Health Ministry: two killed at Touline; multiple wounded in Yater artillery strike; forced evacuation order issued for Deir Aames; six Hezbollah fighters killed in Bint Jbeil-area armed exchange. Cumulative Lebanese casualties since March 2 stand at 2,491 killed and 7,719 wounded per the Health Ministry. PM Netanyahu Friday: Israel 'maintains full freedom of action against any threat' and accused Hezbollah of 'trying to derail talks.' Hezbollah retaliated with rockets, drones, and claimed shootdown of Israeli drone with missile. Yesterday's brief established the Lebanon track as politically de-risked through May 14; the operational reality Saturday is that the kinetic layer never paused. (Sources: Haaretz, Al Jazeera, Siasat, Lebanese Health Ministry, IDF official statement, Al Manar)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED IDF strikes + Lebanese Health Ministry casualty data + Hezbollah retaliation.

signals.content: ISRAELI PRESS SOFTENING ON IRAN TRACK CONTINUES; ISRAELI GOVERNMENT MARGINALISED IN WITKOFF-KUSHNER DISPATCH [REPORTED]. Haaretz softened materially Friday with 'Trump Is Fed Up With the Iran War. That's No Guarantee He Won't Restart It' framing; Saturday's analytical pieces continue that frame. Israeli press is treating the Witkoff-Kushner Pakistan dispatch as a US choice it is observing rather than co-authoring — itself intelligence about Israeli government's marginalisation in the diplomatic track. Israel Hayom continues Mojtaba-medical relay from NYT (April 23 'Injured and isolated, Mojtaba Khamenei rules Iran through a tight inner circle') — pickup consistent with right-of-government press wanting to underscore Iranian leadership weakness. Times of Israel similarly led on Saturday IDF Lebanon strikes without questioning substantive ceasefire validity. Combat-readiness procedures (Channel 12/13/Kan leak Thursday) still active on the Iran track; no Saturday kinetic on Iran. (Sources: Haaretz April 24-25, Times of Israel April 25, Israel Hayom April 23, Al Jazeera April 24-25)
signals.verification: REPORTED tonal shift; CONFIRMED Mojtaba-medical relay across Israel Hayom from NYT.

signals.content: DAY 2 IDF MULTI-LOCATION KINETIC IN SOUTHERN LEBANON; ISRAELI PRESS COMPARTMENTALISED [CONFIRMED IDF action; CONFIRMED tonal pattern]. IDF Sunday strikes in 4+ locations (Bint Jbeil district pair, Tyre district, Nabatieh district two towns); IDF '15+ terrorists eliminated' including 3 in a vehicle 'loaded with weapons.' Two-day cumulative: 10+ locations across 36 hours. Times of Israel separately reported IDF investigation of footage showing soldiers 'damaging solar panels in southern Lebanon town' — discipline issue alongside the kinetic; Haaretz would normally lead on this but is busy with Iran-Pakistan-talks beat, suggesting Times-of-Israel filling Haaretz space. ISRAELI PRESS PATTERN ON IRAN: COMPARTMENTALISED. Haaretz, Times of Israel, and Israel Hayom Sunday all led with the Lebanon Sunday strikes; on Iran, treatment is observant rather than active. Israel Hayom April 25 'All eyes on Pakistan as Iranian delegation leaves' explicitly frames Israel as a watcher. Notably absent: any Israeli senior-source leak about coordination with the US on the post-cancellation Iran kinetic option. Israel's marginalisation in the diplomatic track is being marked by absence rather than active presence. (Sources: Lebanese National News Agency, Al Jazeera, Times of Israel, Haaretz, Israel Hayom)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED Sunday IDF kinetic; CONFIRMED Israeli-press compartmentalised pattern.

signals.content: IDF SGT. IDAN FOOKS KILLED IN HEZBOLLAH DRONE STRIKE — FIRST LETHAL HEZBOLLAH-ON-IDF KINETIC OF POST-EXTENSION PERIOD; HOME FRONT COMMAND GATHERING RESTRICTIONS ACTIVE [CONFIRMED ACTION]. Sunday April 26 morning, Sgt. Idan Fooks, 19, of the 7th Armored Brigade's 77th Battalion, from Petah Tikva, was killed in a Hezbollah explosive drone strike in southern Lebanon. One officer + three troops seriously wounded; one moderate, one light. The two prior fatalities since the November ceasefire were from pre-planted IEDs assessed as not remotely detonated; Sunday's drone is an active, deliberate strike — the qualitative threshold the November architecture was meant to suppress. IDF responded Sunday afternoon with airstrikes and artillery shelling north of the Israeli-declared security zone — Lebanese Health Ministry confirms 14 killed, 37 wounded. Netanyahu accused Hezbollah of 'repeatedly violating the truce' and 'vowed to act forcefully in response.' Israel's Home Front Command issued gathering restrictions in northern border towns from Sunday 22:30 to Monday April 27 20:00 IDT — first time gathering restrictions formally imposed since November ceasefire, itself an operational signal of expected Hezbollah escalation Monday. The substantive ceasefire has collapsed even though legal architecture (May 14) holds. (Sources: Times of Israel, Haaretz, Jerusalem Post, Israel Hayom, JNS, Lebanese National News Agency, Hezbollah official statement)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED ACTION (multi-source IDF announcement + Hezbollah claim + Lebanese Health Ministry casualty data).

signals.content: ISRAELI SENIOR-SOURCE SILENCE ON IRAN POST-CANCELLATION IS DURABLE — ISRAEL OPERATIONALLY MARGINALISED IN DIPLOMATIC TRACK [CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. Trump's Saturday Witkoff-Kushner cancellation + Sunday 'nuclear dust' Reuters quote + Monday Putin-Araghchi Kremlin meeting is precisely the trifecta that should produce a Netanyahu cabinet leak via the usual Haaretz-military-correspondent channels. The silence Sunday-Monday is durable. Haaretz live-blog editorial framing of Trump's 'nuclear dust' line is observant rather than confrontational. Israel is not yet asserting itself on the deal-terms architecture even as the deal-terms architecture moves toward a Russia-custodian model that would substantially diminish Israeli operational influence. The Israel Hayom 'All eyes on Pakistan' positioning has not been corrected Sunday-Monday despite Trump's high-volatility weekend creating an obvious window for Israeli senior-source repositioning. The intelligence-grade read: Israel is being marginalised in the Iran diplomatic track and is not pushing back via its usual leak channels. (Sources: Haaretz April 26-27, Times of Israel April 26-27, Israel Hayom April 26-27, Al Jazeera tracking Israeli press)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED ABSENCE of Israeli senior-source leak.

signals.content: IDF KINETIC DAY 2 — STRUCK 20+ HEZBOLLAH-LINKED SITES; ISRAELI-CIVILIAN-CASUALTY TRIGGER STILL OPEN; ISRAELI SENIOR-SOURCE SILENCE ON IRAN HOLDS [CONFIRMED ACTION + CONFIRMED ABSENCE]. The IDF said Monday it struck more than 20 Hezbollah-linked infrastructure sites across the Beqaa Valley and southern Lebanon (weapons manufacturing, storage, rocket-launch sites). Lebanese MoH: 14 killed, 37 wounded in Sunday strikes; cumulative since March 2 = 2,491 killed, 7,719 wounded. Netanyahu said Hezbollah is 'trying to derail' talks and Israel will respond 'vigorously.' No Hezbollah strike causing Israeli civilian casualty Mon — the 14-day watching trigger (by 2026-05-11) is still OPEN. Lebanon vertical has NOT spread horizontally. On Iran: Haaretz, Times of Israel, Jerusalem Post lead with IDF Lebanon strikes; conspicuously silent on Iran at government level despite Trump deliberating Iran's Hormuz-First proposal. Haaretz military correspondents have NOT leaked any IDF push for direct kinetic on Iran. Israel is being marginalised in the diplomatic track and marking that through absence. UN human rights office: both Israeli strikes AND Hezbollah rocket fire 'may breach international law.' (Sources: Haaretz live blog, Times of Israel, Wikipedia 2026 Lebanon War timeline, Al Jazeera, UN US News)
signals.verification: CONFIRMED IDF strike count + CONFIRMED Israeli civilian-casualty threshold not crossed Mon.

edges.context: Day 59 Sunday. Combat-readiness procedures still active; no Sunday kinetic on Iran. Israel-press observant rather than active on Iran track post-Trump-cancellation. Senior-source silence on coordination with US on post-cancellation Iran kinetic option = Israel marginalised in diplomatic track. Posture unchanged: hold combat-readiness, let Trump own timing.

Day 51. Haaretz April 17: 'Trump hijacked Israeli foreign policy so Netanyahu had no say in Lebanon truce' = sharpest establishment critique of cabinet's diplomatic position. Haaretz military-correspondent sourcing = senior IDF thinking cabinet is suppressing. Times of Israel 'Netanyahu shocked to learn of truce from media' carried into Day 2. IDF 'can't disarm Hezbollah' (Haaretz April 5) supports read that cabinet cannot execute stated objectives. Cabinet Day 2 intact — no walkout.

Day 60 Monday. SUBSTANTIVE CEASEFIRE COLLAPSED. IDF retaliation airstrikes/artillery Sunday afternoon killed 14, wounded 37 in southern Lebanon (Lebanese Health Ministry). Netanyahu 'vowed to act forcefully.' Cumulative Lebanese casualties since March 2: 2,505+ killed, 7,756+ wounded. Home Front Command gathering restrictions through Monday 20:00 IDT — first since November ceasefire. May 14 legal architecture intact but operationally voided.

Day 60 Monday. Hezbollah killed IDF Sgt. Fooks Sunday in explosive-drone attack — first lethal Hezbollah-on-IDF kinetic of post-extension period. The qualitative threshold November architecture was meant to suppress. Hezbollah confirmed responsibility framed as response to ceasefire violations. IDF retaliation followed within hours.
Houthis launched two confirmed attacks on Israel within 24 hours on March 28-29. First Houthi military action against Israel in this war.

trigger_points.condition: Iran strikes Dimona nuclear reactor directly
trigger_points.mechanism: Would be extreme escalation. Could trigger nuclear contamination fears, massive US response, oil spike beyond $130.

trigger_points.condition: Channel 12/13 or Haaretz publishes IDF imminent-strike preparation on Iran track within 72 hours
trigger_points.mechanism: TIO reported Israel coordinating attack plans with US — single-source REPORTED. A follow-on Israeli-military-source leak (Channel 12/13 are typical IDF leak channels; Haaretz military correspondents carry credible senior-officer sourcing) would pre-indicate actual strike at 7-14 day horizon: Brent +$8-12, defense +5-8%. | RESOLVED 2026-04-23 INTO FIRING at 24-hour mark: Channel 12 + Channel 13 + Kan public broadcaster Thursday morning IST coordinated leak. Channel 13: IDF alert level raised 'significantly'; Chief of Staff Eyal Zamir 'instructed the military to prepare for an immediate resumption of fighting and ordered the initiation of combat readiness procedures.' Times of Israel naming the 'apparent coordinated leak' mechanism. Three-channel simultaneity is the deliberate Israeli-defense-establishment notification mechanism. Status MOVED TO ACTIVE — combat readiness procedures initiated is an OPERATIONAL preparation step verified by 3+ primary Hebrew-language outlets.

trigger_points.condition: April 26 IST double expiry — Trump 3-5 day Iran window + Lebanon 10-day ceasefire — without Iran unified proposal AND without Lebanon truce extension
trigger_points.mechanism: Two simultaneous expiries stacked into one weekend — both depend on US mediation; both have parallel military-readiness escalations (IDF combat-readiness procedures on Iran track per Channel 12/13/Kan; Israeli airstrikes continuing on Lebanon track including Lebanese journalist killed Thursday). Hezbollah fired rockets and drones at Israeli forces for first time since truce took effect (Democracy Now). Compound failure path: Lebanon ceasefire lapses + Trump Iran window expires without proposal → Israel resumes Lebanon ops + activates Iran combat-readiness; Hezbollah responds; Iran's IRGC kinetic continues on Hormuz; oil reload to $103-115. Falsifiable: 23:59 IST Sunday April 26. | RESOLVED 2026-04-24 (partial): Lebanon ceasefire EXTENDED 3 weeks to ~May 14 by Trump announcement Thursday — compound-failure path de-risked. Weekend architecture is now SINGLE-expiry (Iran only). Trigger status updated to reflect Lebanon arm no longer firing.
